explanations.6: depends_on lists services that must start before this one; it's how you control the startup order.
explanations.10: condition: service_healthy means Docker will wait until joomladb reports it's actually ready, not just started.
explanations.16: healthcheck defines how Docker checks if the database is really working and accepting connections.
explanations.18: This test command runs inside the container to verify the database is alive—mysqladmin ping is like poking it to see if it responds.
explanations.20: timeout: 20s means Docker will give the ping command up to 20 seconds to complete before considering it a failure.
explanations.22: retries: 10 means Docker will run that test up to 10 times before marking the service as unhealthy if it keeps failing.
